diagrams.title: File Organization Types Comparison
diagrams.description: Three file organization methods showing how records are stored and accessed
elements: Indexed: main file with separate index structure
Direct: hash function mapping keys to buckets
diagrams.purpose: Understand differences between file organization approaches and their access patterns

diagrams.title: Storage Hierarchy Pyramid
diagrams.description: Hierarchical organization of computer storage from fastest to slowest
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the speed-capacity tradeoff in storage systems and understand why caching is important

diagrams.title: Database Normalization Process
diagrams.description: Progressive stages of normalizing a database design
elements: 2NF: no partial dependency on partial key
BCNF: every determinant is a candidate key
diagrams.purpose: Understand how normalization progressively reduces redundancy and improves data integrity
